info.name: Discovery Hub API
info.description: Complete API reference for the Discovery Hub platform.

request.description: Register a new member account. Do NOT include a password — the server generates a secure temporary password and emails it to the provided address. The account will have changedPassword=false, requiring a password change on first login.

Mobile app login (members & workers). Requires deviceId — the first login from a device registers it; subsequent logins must match the registered device or receive 403. Returns requires_password_change: true when a forced password change is pending.
Admin portal login (web). Validates credentials AND verifies the caller has an active Admin record. Returns 403 if the member has no admin access. No device lock — this route is web-first.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. Pass the refresh token in the Authorization header.
Invalidate current session. Returns 204 No Content.
Returns the authenticated user's profile including workerProfile and department if applicable.
Request a 6-digit OTP reset code. Rate-limited (default 3 attempts/hr). Always returns the same message regardless of whether the email exists, to avoid account enumeration.
Verify OTP and set a new password. Marks the OTP as used, invalidates the current session, and sends a confirmation email. The user must log in fresh after this.

Self-service device reset step 1. Rate-limited (default 3 per 24 h per email). Sends a 6-digit OTP to the registered email address. The newDeviceId submitted here is locked in — the OTP cannot be used to register a different device.

Self-service device reset step 2. Verifies the OTP, updates member.deviceId to the newDeviceId submitted in the request step, invalidates all active sessions, and sends a confirmation email. Member must log in fresh from the new device.

item.name: Members
item.description: All endpoints require ADMIN role unless noted.

query.description: Case-insensitive match on firstname, lastname, email or phone
Filter by worker status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE (optional)

request.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.
Creates a WorkerProfile for the member, assigns them to a department and sets role to WORKER. departmentId is required; profession and yearJoinedWorkforce are optional.
Removes the WorkerProfile and resets the member's role back to MEMBER. Returns 204 No Content.

Update a worker's profile fields. All fields are optional. Pass `"secondaryDepartmentId": null` to remove the secondary department assignment. Omitting `secondaryDepartmentId` entirely leaves the current value unchanged. ADMIN only.
Activate or deactivate a member. status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE. Returns 204.
Generates a new random password and emails it to the member. The member will need to change it on next login.

Admin (MEMBERS_WRITE): Remove the registered deviceId for a member and invalidate all active sessions. This resets the one-device-per-member lock, allowing the member to log in from any device. Responds with 204 No Content.
Grants admin portal access to an existing member. Requires ADMIN_WRITE permission. Sends a welcome-admin email to the member with their email address and the portal login URL.
Soft-revokes admin access (sets isActive=false). Requires ADMIN_WRITE permission. Sends an account-deactivated notification email to the affected member.

Admin (AUDIT_READ): Paginated audit log. Supports optional filters: action, actorId, targetId, dateFrom, dateTo. Returns paginated AuditLog records with the actor relation loaded (actor name/email included).

Any authenticated user (MEMBER or WORKER) can check in. Workers MUST include location coordinates. Members may omit location. Location distance is validated if ENFORCE_DISTANCE_CHECK=true. One record per member per event — checking in to any slot of an event covers the whole event. Members are always PRESENT; workers are PRESENT or LATE depending on workerLateOffsetSeconds. Duplicate check-ins return 409 (enforced at DB level via unique constraint on (member, event)).

response.name: 409 Already checked in
response.body: { "statusCode": 409, "message": "You have already checked in for this event." }

response.name: 400 Worker missing location
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Workers must provide their location to check in." }

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's own attendance records. Available to all roles.
WORKER only (department lead). Returns attendance for their department for a given slot.
Returns PRESENT/LATE/ABSENT/ON_LEAVE counts for a single service slot.
Returns top workers ranked by present+late count within the time window.
Returns attendance records for the caller's department for a specific event. Includes all service slots within that event. WORKER only (resolves the caller's department from their profile).
Admin correction of a specific attendance record. Requires ATTENDANCE_WRITE permission. Body: { status: PRESENT | LATE | ABSENT | ON_LEAVE }.

Confirms that the authenticated member attended the event online. Valid only while the online window is open (ONLINE_CHECKIN_WINDOW_HOURS after notification was sent). Updates the ABSENT record to ATTENDED_ONLINE.

response.name: 404 No record
response.body: { "statusCode": 404, "message": "No attendance record found for this event" }

request.description: Create a single non-recurring event. endDate defaults to eventDate if omitted. Slot times must fall within [eventDate 00:00, endDate 23:59]. Slots are auto-sorted ASC and validated for no overlap.
Minimal single-event example — one slot with a configId. At least one serviceSlot is required; slots without a configId will fail at check-in time.

Creates one event record per recurrence date up to recurrenceEndDate (max 1 year ahead). serviceSlots are required (at least one) and must include a configId. The same slot template is stamped on every occurrence — all share the same config. Delete future occurrences in bulk via DELETE /events/recurring/:recurringEventId.

Updates event metadata. When `serviceSlots` is provided (min 1 entry), ALL existing slots are replaced with the new ones — include `configId` on each slot to preserve config linkage. Omit `serviceSlots` entirely to leave existing slots unchanged. ADMIN only.

query.description: YYYY-MM-DD — filter events on or after this date
YYYY-MM-DD — filter events on or before this date
true — return only future events (eventDate >= today)

request.description: ADMIN only. Cannot delete past events.

variable.description: The shared recurringEventId UUID from the event series

request.description: ADMIN only. Deletes all future events in a recurring series (today and after).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Default Sunday Config",
  "description": "Standard check-in config for Sunday services",
  "defaultVenueId": "{{venueId}}",
  "workerCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -5400,
  "workerLateOffsetSeconds": 0,
  "memberCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -1800,
  "checkinStopOffsetSeconds": 3600,
  "allowedDistanceInMeters": 150
}

request.description: Offsets are in seconds relative to slot startTime. Negative = before slot starts. Constraint: workerLateOffset > workerCheckinStartOffset and checkinStopOffset > workerLateOffset. defaultVenueId is required — create a Venue first and reference it here.
Fails if any service slot is still assigned to this config.
Returns the list of all valid DepartmentKeyEnum values. Use this to populate key-selection dropdowns when creating or updating a department. No auth required beyond a valid JWT.
Public (JWT only). Returns paginated list of departments.

ADMIN only. The optional `key` field links this department to a module-access category (DepartmentKeyEnum). Multiple departments may share the same key. Omit `key` (or pass null) if the department does not gate any module. Example keys: SUNDAY_SCHOOL, CHILDREN_CHURCH, MEDIA.
ADMIN only. All fields are optional. The `key` field can be updated to change module access. Set to null to remove module gating.
Assigns a worker as department head or assistant. type: 'head' | 'assistant'. The worker must have this department as their primary department to be assigned as HOD.
Paginated list of all workers whose primary department is this one. Each worker entry includes a `leadRole` field ("head" | "assistant" | null). ADMIN only.

Returns aggregated stats for the department the caller leads (HOD or D-HOD): total/active/inactive worker counts, 30-day attendance %, and workers currently on approved or pending leave. WORKER (HOD/D-HOD) only — throws 403 if caller is not a department lead.
WORKER only. Dates must be in format: YYYY-MM-DD (date only, no time). dateTo must be on or after dateFrom. Creates a PENDING leave request.
ADMIN only. status: APPROVED | REJECTED.
WORKER only. Can only delete own PENDING leave requests.
WORKER only. Returns leave requests from members in the same department.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "New Believers Class",
  "type": "BELIEVERS",
  "description": "Foundation class for new members",
  "facilitatorId": "{{memberId}}",
  "startDate": "2026-07-01",
  "endDate": "2026-09-30"
}

request.description: type: BELIEVERS | BAPTISMAL | WORKERS_IN_TRAINING | BIBLE_COLLEGE
ADMIN only (CLASSES_WRITE). Returns 400 if any members are currently enrolled with status IN_PROGRESS. Complete or cancel all active enrolments before deleting.

response.name: 400 Already enrolled
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Member is already enrolled in this class" }

request.description: status: IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETED | CANCELLED
Returns all class enrollments for the authenticated user.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Church Picnic — July 20th",
  "body": "Join us for our annual church picnic at Tarkwa Bay Beach. Bring your family!",
  "audience": "ALL",
  "publishedAt": "2026-07-10T08:00:00.000Z",
  "expiresAt": "2026-07-21T00:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description: audience: ALL | WORKERS_ONLY | DEPARTMENT | INDIVIDUAL. If audience=DEPARTMENT, departmentId is required. If audience=INDIVIDUAL, targetMemberId (UUID) is required. publishedAt defaults to now if omitted.
Sends a private announcement visible only to the specified member. targetMemberId is required when audience=INDIVIDUAL.

query.description: Required for workers to receive department-targeted announcements (optional for members)

request.description: Returns visible, non-expired announcements for the current user's role. MEMBER sees ALL. WORKER sees ALL + WORKERS_ONLY + their department. Expired announcements are excluded.
type path param: child_naming | child_dedication | marriage
type field is required to identify the note type. Other fields are optional (partial update).

query.description: Start date YYYY-MM-DD (optional, defaults to 30 days ago)
End date YYYY-MM-DD (optional, defaults to today)

item.name: Venues
item.description: Named, reusable venue records. Create a venue once; reference its ID in event configs (defaultVenueId) or individual service slots (venueOverrideId). ADMIN: create, update, delete. Any authenticated user: list, get by ID.

request.description: Creates a new venue. The test script auto-saves the returned id to the venueId collection variable. name must be unique. latitude and longitude are required.
Any authenticated user. Returns all venues paginated, ordered by name.
All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.
Fails with 400 if the venue is referenced as a defaultVenue by any event config. Slot-level venueOverrides pointing to this venue are automatically cleared (SET NULL).

response.name: 400 In use
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Cannot delete this venue — it is referenced by one or more event configs or slots." }

request.description: Any authenticated user. Returns profile, streak, rank, period stats, recent attendance, upcoming events, and class enrollments.

WORKER or ADMIN role. Includes streak, rank, period stats, and isDepartmentLead flag. If isDepartmentLead=true, also includes departmentLeadDetails with departmentAttendancePercentage and pending leave count.

ADMIN only. Returns total member/worker/admin counts, check-ins today, workerAttendancePercentage, congregationAttendancePercentage, departmentAttendanceSummary (per-department breakdown), newMemberRegistrationsTrend, weekly trend, top absent workers, members not seen recently, upcoming events, and pending leave count.

item.name: Sunday School
item.description: Session-based attendance for permanent Sunday School classes. Access requires ADMIN role, a worker whose primary or secondary department has key=SUNDAY_SCHOOL, or the appointed class teacher. Self-mark endpoints are open to any enrolled member when the window is open.

request.description: ADMIN or SS-dept worker. teacherId is optional — links an existing member as the class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or the class teacher. All fields are optional.
ADMIN only.
Any authenticated user.
Any authenticated user.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Permanently assigns a member to the class. Assignment is unique per (member, class).
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. sessionDate must be unique per class. selfMarkOpen defaults to false.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Flips selfMarkOpen between true and false. When open, enrolled members can self-mark their attendance.
Any authenticated user. Caller must be enrolled in the class and the session's selfMarkOpen must be true. Marks the caller PRESENT with markedByTeacher=false.

response.name: 400 Window closed
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Self-mark window is not open for this session." }

response.name: 400 Not enrolled
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "You are not enrolled in this class." }

request.description: ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Upserts attendance for multiple members in a single call. status: PRESENT | ABSENT | EXCUSED. Sets markedByTeacher=true.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Returns the full attendance roster for the session including members with no record yet.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Paginated list of all sessions for a class, newest first. Requires SS department membership or class teacher role. WORKER/ADMIN.
Returns a single session with its class loaded. WORKER/ADMIN.
Deletes the session and all attendance records for it (CASCADE). Only the class teacher or SS staff can delete. WORKER/ADMIN.

item.name: Children Church
item.description: Security check-in/check-out system for children. Access requires ADMIN role or a worker whose primary or secondary department has key=CHILDREN_CHURCH. Age group and class group management is ADMIN-only.

request.description: ADMIN only. Age ranges are in months (inclusive). displayOrder controls sort order in listings.
ADMIN only. All fields are optional.

ADMIN only (CHILDREN_CHURCH_WRITE). Returns 400 if any child profiles are assigned to this age group or any of its class groups. Reassign or remove all affected children first. Deleting an age group also cascades to all its class groups.
Any authenticated user. Returns age groups ordered by displayOrder.

ADMIN only. Iterates all registered children and reassigns each to the correct ageGroup and classGroup based on their current date of birth and the latest age-group rules. Run this after changing age-group boundaries.
ADMIN only. capacity and teacherNote are optional.
ADMIN only.
ADMIN only (CHILDREN_CHURCH_WRITE). Returns 400 if any child profiles are currently assigned to this class group. Reassign or remove them first.

query.description: Optional. Filter class groups by age group UUID.

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. ageGroup and classGroup are automatically assigned from dateOfBirth. photoUrl, specialNotes, and registeredByMemberId are optional.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. All fields optional.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns full profile including ageGroup, classGroup, and guardians.
Paginated check-in/check-out history for a specific child, newest first. Includes the worker who performed each check-in (checkedInBy). WORKER/ADMIN.
Lists children, optionally filtered by name and/or classGroupId. Omit both filters to return all children. WORKER/ADMIN.

ADMIN or CC-dept worker. relationship: MOTHER | FATHER | GRANDPARENT | SIBLING | UNCLE | AUNT | FAMILY_FRIEND | OTHER. email and memberId are optional. Pickup notification emails are sent to guardian.email ?? guardian.member.email.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Generates a unique 6-char pickupCode and emails it to all registered guardians. serviceSlotId, droppedOffByGuardianId, and droppedOffByName are optional.

variable.description: The 6-char pickup code printed on the child's tag

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns the matching ChildCheckIn record including child and guardian details for the pickup verification step.

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body: { "statusCode": 404, "message": "No active check-in found for code ABC123" }

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Validates the pickup code, sets status to CHECKED_OUT, and records the pickup time. pickedUpByGuardianId is optional if a name is sufficient.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Sets status to FLAGGED and records the reason. Flagged check-ins require manual resolution.

query.description: Optional. Filter by class group UUID.

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns all check-ins with status CHECKED_IN or FLAGGED, optionally filtered by classGroupId.
ADMIN only. Returns all check-in records (any status) for a given service slot.

item.name: Birthday
item.description: Birthday wish wall. Any authenticated user can send a wish and read their own wall. Admins can read any member's wall. A cron job fires daily at 06:00 to create an announcement and send birthday emails. Wishes are permanent (they outlive the midnight-expiring announcement).

request.description: Any authenticated user. Send a birthday wish to a member. Rules: cannot wish yourself; one wish per sender per recipient per calendar year (enforced at DB level); max 500 characters; rate-limited to WISH_DAILY_LIMIT (default 20) wishes per sender per day.

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "message": "Happy birthday! Wishing you a year full of God's blessings and joy.",
  "year": 2026,
  "sender": { "id": "uuid", "firstname": "John", "lastname": "Doe" },
  "createdAt": "2026-06-11T07:15:00.000Z"
}

response.name: 400 Self-wish
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "You cannot send a wish to yourself" }

response.name: 400 Duplicate
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "You have already sent a wish to this member this year" }

response.name: 429 Rate limit
response.body: { "statusCode": 429, "message": "You have reached the daily limit for birthday wishes" }

query.description: Optional. Filter wishes by calendar year (defaults to current year).

request.description: Any authenticated user. Returns all birthday wishes the caller received, newest first. Optionally filter by year.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: ADMIN only. Read any member's birthday wish wall. Returns all wishes received by the specified member, optionally filtered by year.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Event Reminders
item.description: Per-slot, configurable reminders. Each reminder fires once (before the slot's startTime) via a 15-minute cron and creates an in-app announcement plus sends a batch email. ADMIN only. Audience: ALL | WORKERS_ONLY | DEPARTMENT (INDIVIDUAL is rejected). Preset intervals: 15m | 30m | 1h | 3h | 24h | 48h.

request.description: ADMIN only. Creates a reminder for a service slot. intervalPreset: 15m | 30m | 1h | 3h | 24h | 48h. audience defaults to ALL. If audience=DEPARTMENT, departmentId is required. The reminder fires once when startTime - preset_minutes <= now (cron checks every 15 min). The (serviceSlot, intervalPreset) pair must be unique per slot.

response.name: 400 INDIVIDUAL audience
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "INDIVIDUAL audience is not supported for slot reminders" }

response.name: 409 Duplicate preset
response.body: { "statusCode": 409, "message": "A reminder with this interval preset already exists for this slot" }

request.description: ADMIN only. Creates a department-targeted reminder. The reminder email and in-app announcement are sent only to active workers in the specified department.
ADMIN only. Returns all reminders configured for a service slot, ordered by creation time. Each entry shows the preset, audience, enabled status, and lastSentAt (null = not yet fired).

ADMIN only. Partially updates a reminder. All fields are optional: intervalPreset, audience, departmentId, enabled. Set enabled=false to soft-disable without deleting (the cron will skip disabled reminders).
ADMIN only. Permanently deletes the reminder. Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: Enums
item.description: Utility endpoints that return all enum values with human-readable labels. Frontends use these to populate dropdowns without hardcoding enum values.

request.description: Returns all enum values with labels for the mobile app: attendance statuses, member roles, gender, marital status, worker status, leave status, announcement audiences, church class types, enrollment statuses, department keys, department lead types, guardian relationships, child check-in statuses, note types, event recurrence patterns, and reminder interval presets. Requires a valid JWT.
AdminGuard (ADMIN_READ). Returns admin-specific enum values with labels: all AdminPermission values. Used by the admin portal to populate role permission checkboxes.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Downloads a 3-sheet Excel workbook: Tithe Template (headers), Instructions, Sample row.

formdata.description: Excel .xlsx file with columns: Email, Amount, Payment Date (YYYY-MM-DD), Reference (optional), Bank Name (optional)

request.description: AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Validates headers synchronously; dispatches processing to Bull queue. Returns the created TitheUploadBatch.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Paginated list of all upload batches.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Get a single batch with stats.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Requeue a FAILED batch using its stored row data. Resets status to PENDING and re-dispatches the Bull job. Returns 204 No Content.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Rows from any batch where no member matched the email. status defaults to PENDING; pass MATCHED or DISMISSED to review resolved rows.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Manually links a PENDING unmatched row to a member, creating a TitheRecord and marking the row as MATCHED.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Marks a PENDING unmatched row as DISMISSED — intentionally ignored (e.g. test entry, refund, person not in the system). No TitheRecord is created.

AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Rows that matched a member but duplicated an existing TitheRecord by (memberId, paymentDate, amount). status defaults to PENDING; pass CONFIRMED_VALID or REJECTED to review resolved ones.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Approves the dispute and creates a TitheRecord.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Rejects the dispute; the row is ignored.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Lists all member-submitted tithe payment proofs. Optional `status` filter.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Sets proof status to CONFIRMED and emails the member. Returns 204.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Sets proof status to DECLINED, stores financeNote, and emails the member. Returns 204.
JwtAuthGuard. Returns the authenticated member's own tithe records (paginated).
JwtAuthGuard. Generates a PDF tithe statement and emails it to the caller's registered email address.

formdata.description: Receipt image or PDF. Max 2 MB.

request.description: JwtAuthGuard. Upload a tithe payment proof (multipart, field: file, max 2 MB). Creates a TithePaymentProof with status PENDING. Finance team is notified by email.
JwtAuthGuard. Returns the authenticated member's own tithe payment proofs (paginated).
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Returns all finance categories sorted by name.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Creates a new expense category. Name must be unique.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Updates an existing category.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Paginated list of all finance requests. Filterable by status (PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED) and categoryId.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_READ). Get a single finance request with full relations.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Approves a PENDING request. Notifies the HOD by email.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Rejects a PENDING request. Requires a rejectionReason. Notifies the HOD by email.
AdminGuard (FINANCE_WRITE). Uploads payment proof to Cloudinary and attaches the URL to an APPROVED request. Notifies the HOD by email.
RolesGuard (WORKER). Returns all finance categories for use in the request creation form.

formdata.description: Must match the caller's own department

formdata.value: Purchase of new projector for services

request.description: RolesGuard (WORKER) — HOD only. Raises a finance request for the caller's department. Server verifies the caller is a department lead and that departmentId matches their department. Notifies all FINANCE_WRITE admins by email.
RolesGuard (WORKER) — HOD only. Returns paginated requests for the caller's own department.

RolesGuard (WORKER) — HOD only. Returns a single finance request belonging to the caller's own department. Includes proofUrl once payment proof has been attached. Returns 404 if the request belongs to a different department.
Register a first-time visitor. Caller must be a WORKER in the FOLLOW_UP department. Auto-creates a FollowUpTask assigned via round-robin.
List follow-up tasks assigned to the authenticated FOLLOW_UP-dept worker. Optional ?status= filter.
Update a follow-up task. Caller must be the assigned worker. Optionally adds a note.

response.name: 404 Not found
response.body: { "statusCode": 404, "message": "Task not found or not assigned to you" }

request.description: Register a first-timer from the admin portal. Requires FOLLOW_UP_WRITE permission.
List all first-timers. Requires FOLLOW_UP_READ permission. Optional ?eventId= filter.
List all follow-up tasks. Requires FOLLOW_UP_READ. Optional filters: status, type.
Reassign a follow-up task to a different FOLLOW_UP-dept worker. Requires FOLLOW_UP_WRITE permission.

response.name: 400 Not in FOLLOW_UP dept
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Target worker must be in the Follow-Up department" }

request.description: Bulk update status for multiple follow-up tasks. Requires FOLLOW_UP_WRITE permission.

Pastoral aggregate report. Requires FOLLOW_UP_READ permission. Optional ?from= and ?to= (ISO date strings) filter by period; omit both for all-time stats. Returns: first-timer totals, source breakdown, wants-to-join counts, task status/outcome breakdown, overdue count, conversion rate, per-worker performance, per-event first-timer counts.
